page: Microsoft Azure E96d_v5 is an EDv5-series virtual machine configured with 96 vCPUs and 672.0 GB of RAM, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The underlying hardware features an Intel Xeon 8370C processor with an x86_64 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 2.8 GHz across 48 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. This instance includes 3,686 GB of local storage, but does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core CPU and Geekbench multi-core scores placing in the top 25% tier. Memory performance is also highly rated, showing strong read and write bandwidth for large uncached blocks, alongside a top 10% tier ranking in Passmark CPU encryption tests. However, single-core performance remains in the average tier, as demonstrated by stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core results.

While the server excels at multi-threaded tasks, such as bzip3 multi-threaded decompression and static web serving throughput, it exhibits performance tradeoffs in large-scale language model execution. LLM inference for text generation using a large 70B model falls into the bottom 25% tier, generating 2.68 tokens per second. Consequently, this server is best suited for memory-intensive workloads, database operations, Redis deployments, and multi-threaded data processing rather than large-scale LLM text generation.
description: Microsoft Azure E96d_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 96 vCPUs and 672.0 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor with 48 physical cores and hyper-threading. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance includes 3,686 GB of local storage and lacks GPU acceleration. Performance benchmarks show strong multi-core capabilities, with top 25% rankings in multi-core CPU execution, memory bandwidth, and static web serving throughput, as well as a top 10% ranking in CPU encryption. Conversely, single-core performance and large-scale LLM text generation represent notable tradeoffs, with the latter falling into the bottom 25% tier. This resource density makes the instance highly suitable for memory-intensive database operations, Redis caching, and multi-threaded enterprise applications where high local storage and memory capacity are required.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E96d_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine with 96 vCPUs, 672 GB RAM, and 3,686 GB local storage, delivering strong multi-core and database performance for enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E96d_v5 features 96 vCPUs, 672 GB RAM, and 3,686 GB local storage, optimized for memory-intensive database workloads.
tagline: A high-capacity memory and storage virtual machine designed for demanding database operations, multi-threaded processing, and enterprise caching workloads.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs and 672 GB RAM powered by Intel Xeon 8370C processors
Includes 3,686 GB of local storage for high-speed data access
Strong multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database benchmark performance
Top 10% tier performance in Passmark CPU encryption tests
Tradeoffs include average single-core speeds and weak large LLM text generation
Best suited for relational databases, Redis caching, and multi-threaded data processing
